name: 755CreditScore.com
description_short: Houston-based credit repair company offering dispute services and credit consulting to remove negative items from credit reports across all three bureaus.
description_long:
755CreditScore.com is a credit repair consulting service based in Houston, Texas, claiming over 10 years of experience and 4,500+ satisfied clients. The company positions itself as a credit restoration specialist focused on helping consumers understand their credit reports and dispute inaccuracies or negative items with the three major credit bureaus (Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion). Their core business model centers on analyzing client credit profiles, identifying problematic accounts, and generating dispute letters to challenge negative items on credit reports.

The company offers credit repair consulting services including analysis of derogatory accounts, individualized improvement plans, and dispute letter generation for specific issues like collections, late payments, charge-offs, judgments, foreclosures, and identity theft. They also mention a "Credit Boost" service and partner offerings for secured credit cards. Services are available in multiple languages (English, Spanish, German, and Vietnamese). The company provides initial consultations to develop client-specific strategies, with fees quoted based on the scope of account verification and dispute work needed.

The firm emphasizes personalized service and honest consulting, positioning itself as an advocate for consumer rights. They explicitly state they cannot guarantee specific credit score improvements by particular dates, but reference typical timelines based on past client experiences. The company provides educational content about credit reports, charge-offs, late payments, and FICO scoring, and maintains a Google review presence they market as proof of client satisfaction.

A notable limitation is the company's lack of specific detail about methodologies, success rates, pricing structures, or regulatory compliance disclosures on their website. While they claim honest service, the site contains generic marketing language typical of credit repair companies and lacks transparency about what specific actions they take, whether they are members of professional associations like the National Association of Credit Services Organizations (NACSO), or how they comply with the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA).

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Program
features: Credit report analysis across all three bureaus
Dispute filing for inaccurate and unverifiable items

pros: 10+ years claimed experience in credit repair industry
Multilingual support in English, Spanish, German, and Vietnamese
Claims 4,500+ satisfied clients with Google reviews available
Offers customized analysis including derogatory account identification before charging fees
Generates specific dispute letters for collections, inquiries, and late payments
Serves all three major credit bureaus (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Provides free initial consultation and credit report analysis
cons: No pricing information disclosed on website; fees only provided during consultation
Makes no guarantees about credit score improvement or timelines, limiting accountability
Website lacks detail about specific dispute methodologies or success rates by issue type
No visible compliance disclosures regarding Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA) requirements
Generic marketing language without third-party certifications or professional association memberships listed
best_for: Houston-area consumers with collections, charge-offs, or judgments on their credit reports
Non-English speakers needing multilingual credit repair support
Individuals wanting personalized analysis before committing to credit repair services
Consumers disputing late payments, foreclosures, or identity theft items
diagnosis: 755CreditScore.com is appropriate for consumers with negative credit report items who want personalized analysis and dispute letter generation, particularly Spanish, German, or Vietnamese speakers in the Houston area. The main caveat is the absence of pricing transparency, success rate data, and regulatory compliance documentation—standard omissions that reflect the broader credit repair industry's lack of accountability, making it essential consumers research independently before engaging. Consumers comparing credit repair companies should also consider whether credit monitoring services, secured credit cards, or credit counseling might address their needs alongside or instead of paid credit repair services.
services: Credit report analysis and account identification
Dispute letter generation for negative items
Judgment and public record dispute support
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
